proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=97, 93.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +41.1pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8690 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)

Portfolio is 72/73 (98.6%) concentrated in UP_TRI signal type — when Bear regime returns and this cohort activates, sizing discipline (TAKE_SMALL cap) is essential to avoid further signal-type concentration
R-multiple 0.61 remains under D-8 review per project_anchor §7; sustained Day-6 forced-exit dominance could erode the cohort's effective edge even with high WR
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=97, wr=93.7%, edge +41.1pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=97, Wilson lower 0.869, +41.1pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Bull (shifting, day 1), so the cohort is dormant; portfolio is heavily UP_TRI/Bull-Choppy with no flagged concentration triggered (0.63 below 0.80), and Bear-gated promotion does not compound active exposure. Consistent with continuous prior approvals (2026-04-29 through 2026-06-28) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: BULL_PROXY × Bear: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=BULL_PROXY, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +30.8pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.98 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
approval would propagate redundant to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +30.8pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +30.8pp edge over BULL_PROXY baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.98 (<1.0). Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Bull (shifting, day 1), so the cohort is dormant; portfolio Bear exposure is 0 with no concentration trigger fired (observed 0.6301 below 0.8 threshold), so promotion does not compound concentration risk and may diversify when Bear re-emerges. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-06-19 through 06-28) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_signal_type = UP_TRI (98.6%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_signal_type = UP_TRI; recommended action: Pause new entries in signal_type=UP_TRI until signal mix diversifies (target <85% share) or until a validated (Tier M/S) cohort emerges for the top concentrated cells (Bank, Energy). Allow natural exit attrition to reduce monoculture.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A Bull→Bear or sharp Choppy→Bear regime flip would simultaneously turn the entire 73-position directional-long book against the trader, with no validated edge data to justify riding through drawdown — cohort tiers are Candidate, so historical WR/edge is not statistically established.

evidence: Book is fully directional long (UP_TRI + BULL_PROXY = 73/73 = 100%)
Top cell UP_TRI×Bull×Bank at 15.07% — matched cohort sector=Bank__signal_type=UP_TRI is Candidate tier (n=64), unvalidated edge
Second cell UP_TRI×Bull×Energy at 10.96% — matched cohort sector=Energy__signal_type=UP_TRI is Candidate tier (n=30), unvalidated edge
Regime mix Bull 63.0% / Choppy 37.0% — no single regime above 0.80, but a regime flip would unwind the long-heavy book simultaneously
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in signal_type=UP_TRI until signal mix diversifies (target <85% share) or until a validated (Tier M/S) cohort emerges for the top concentrated cells (Bank, Energy). Allow natural exit attrition to reduce monoculture.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A Bull→Bear or sharp Choppy→Bear regime flip would simultaneously turn the entire 73-position directional-long book against the trader, with no validated edge data to justify riding through drawdown — cohort tiers are Candidate, so historical WR/edge is not statistically established.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: UP_TRI dominates the signal mix at 72/73 = 98.6% (well above the 0.85 threshold), making the book a near-monoculture directional long. The matched cohorts for both top cells (UP_TRI×Bull×Bank and UP_TRI×Bull×Energy) sit only at Candidate tier — unvalidated edge. While regime concentration (Bull 63.0%) and the top cells (Bank 15.07%, Energy 10.96%) are within their respective thresholds, the signal-type monoculture on unvalidated cohorts is the actionable risk.
